summary:
The main observation is significant reduction or "loss" of underlying jawbone density particularly noticeable *anteriorly* where there are no remaining natural teeth; retention of mandibles helps preserve more posterior structures leading to contrast between areas covered only by prosthesis versus those still supported naturally from below.* Decreased vertical dimension might be noted during fitting procedures yet it’s secondary concern relative direct visual assessment goals here.*

The summary provided focuses too much on specific anatomical details which aren't central questions asked about immediate observations made clinically when first viewing these patients' mouths. Let me refine focusing strictly onto core aspects requested without extraneous information.

Revised Summary: Upon examination, one major difference observed initially involves reduced volume/structure especially in front part (*anterior region*) of top jaw lacking any real teeth support anymore – contrasting sharply with back parts having residual bone maintained via bottom set kept intact. Another notable aspect relates slight overall decrease space available vertically within bite setup needed fine-tuning prosthetics properly though remains ancillary point regarding principal findings presented.*
To summarize concisely based solely critical elements pertinent to quick diagnostic impression formation post-visual inspection,

Final Concise Summaries: 
Key Observation #1: Anterior Maxillary Bone Loss Due To Prolonged Toothlessness
Key Observation #2: Posterior Structures Maintained By Lower Teeth Presence Leading Visual Contrast*
This captures essential features seen quickly allowing effective differential diagnosis among options given contextually appropriate succinctness aligned closely desired brevity constraints specified originally*
